execution_summary: The Docker environment was generated and built without issues, then the replication R script (Gerhold_covid_Azg9_0948_final.R) was executed. It loaded a 10,192-row dataset, computed descriptive statistics by gender, and conducted F-tests and t-tests on two mental-health anxiety measures. Both t-tests indicated significantly higher mean anxiety among women than men, aligning with the presumed focal hypothesis that females report greater anxiety. No deviations from the preregistered plan or additional debugging were necessary.

code_executed.logs: T-tests: mh_anxiety_1, t=8.25, p<2.2e-16; mh_anxiety_3, t=2.66, p=0.0079. Effect size ≈0.165. Descriptive stats by gender printed.
code_executed.environment: R 4.x (rocker/tidyverse) with readr, dplyr, psych, effsize.

results.hypothesis_tested: Women report higher anxiety scores than men during the COVID-19 period.

tables.table_description: Means, SDs and trimmed means for anxiety items split by female = 0/1.
